sections.label: Section 1.1260
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐγὼ τόνδʼ , ὃς πρὸς ἑσπέραν φέρει .
sections.translation: And I on this one , that leads westward .

sections.label: Section 1.1261
sections.original: δόχμιά νυν κόρας διάφερʼ ὀμμάτων .
sections.translation: Throw a glance sideways .

sections.label: Section 1.1262
sections.original: ἐκεῖθεν ἐνθάδʼ , εἶτα παλινσκοπιὰν
sections.translation: Here and there , then we are looking back again ,

sections.label: Section 1.1265
sections.original: ἔχομεν , ὡς θροεῖς .
sections.translation: as you tell us .

sections.label: Section 1.1266
sections.original: ἑλίσσετέ νυν βλέφαρα ,
sections.translation: Cast your eyes around , let them see everything , through your tresses .

sections.label: Section 1.1268
sections.original: ὅδε τις ἐν τρίβῳ προσέρχεται . τίς ὅδʼ ἄρʼ ἀμ-
sections.translation: Who is that on the road ? Who is this

sections.label: Section 1.1270
sections.original: φὶ μέλαθρον πολεῖ σὸν ἀγρότας ἀνήρ ;
sections.translation: country-man wandering round your house ?

sections.label: Section 1.1271
sections.original: ἀπωλόμεσθʼ ἄρʼ , ὦ φίλαι · κεκρυμμένους
sections.translation: Ah ! friends , we are ruined ; he will at once reveal to our enemies the armed ambush .

sections.label: Section 1.1273
sections.original: ἄφοβος ἔχε · κενός , ὦ φίλα ,
sections.translation: ( Reconnoitring . Calm your fears ; the road is not occupied , as you think , my dear .

sections.label: Section 1.1275
sections.original: τί δέ ; τὸ σὸν βέβαιον ἔτι μοι μένει ;
sections.translation: ( Turning to the other watchers .) Well ? Is your side still secure ? Give me a good report , if the space before the court-yard is deserted .

sections.label: Section 1.1278
sections.original: καλῶς τά γʼ ἐνθένδʼ . ἀλλὰ τἀπὶ σοῦ σκόπει ·
sections.translation: All goes well here ; look to your own watch , for no Danaid is approaching us .

sections.label: Section 1.1280
sections.original: ἐς ταὐτὸν ἥκεις · καὶ γὰρ οὐδὲ τῇδʼ ὄχλος .
sections.translation: Your report agrees with mine ; there is no noise here either .

sections.label: Section 1.1281
sections.original: φέρε νυν ἐν πύλαισιν ἀκοὰν βάλω .
sections.translation: Well then , I will listen in the gateway .

sections.label: Section 1.1284
sections.original: τί μέλλεθʼ οἱ κατʼ οἶκον ἐν ἡσυχίᾳ
sections.translation: ( Calling through the door .) You within the house , why are you delaying to spill your victim ’ s blood ,

sections.label: Section 1.1285
sections.original: σφάγια φοινίσσειν ;
sections.translation: now that all is quiet ?

sections.label: Section 1286
sections.original: οὐκ εἰσακούουσʼ · ὦ τάλαινʼ ἐγὼ κακῶν .
sections.translation: They do not hear ; alas for my troubles ! Can it be that her beauty has blunted their swords ?

sections.label: Section 1288
sections.original: τάχα τις Ἀργείων ἔνοπλος ὁρμήσας
sections.translation: Soon some Argive in full armor , hurrying

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: ποδὶ βοηδρόμῳ μέλαθρα προσμείξει .
sections.translation: to her rescue , will attack the palace .

sections.label: Section 1291
sections.original: σκέψασθέ νυν ἄμεινον · οὐχ ἕδρας ἀγών ·
sections.translation: Keep a better look-out ; it is not a contest of sitting still ; turn about , some here , some there .

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: ἀμείβω κέλευθον σκοποῦσα πάντῃ .
sections.translation: I am looking everywhere in turn along the road .

sections.label: Section 1296
sections.original: ἰὼ Πελασγὸν Ἄργος , ὄλλυμαι κακῶς .
sections.translation: Oh , Pelasgian Argos ! I am being foully murdered .

sections.label: Section 1297
sections.original: — ἠκούσαθʼ ; ἅνδρες χεῖρʼ ἔχουσιν ἐν φόνῳ .
sections.translation: Did you hear ? The men have put their hand to the slaughter .

sections.label: Section 1298
sections.original: — Ἑλένης τὸ κώκυμʼ ἐστίν , ὡς ἀπεικάσαι .
sections.translation: It is Helen screaming , at a guess .

sections.label: Section 1299
sections.original: ὦ Διός , ὦ Διὸς ἀέναον κράτος ,
sections.translation: O eternal might of Zeus , of Zeus , only come to help my friends !

sections.label: Section 1301
sections.original: Μενέλαε , θνῄσκω · σὺ δὲ παρών μʼ οὐκ ὠφελεῖς .
sections.translation: Menelaus , I am dying , but you do not help me , though you are near .

sections.label: Section 1302
sections.original: φονεύετε , καίνετε ,
sections.translation: Slay her , kill her , destroy her ! Stab with your twin double-edged swords

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: τὰν λιποπάτορα λιπόγαμον , ἃ
sections.translation: the woman who left her father , left her husband , and killed so many of the men of Hellas , slain beside the river-bank , where tears rained down , by the iron darts

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: φὶ τὰς Σκαμάνδρου δίνας .
sections.translation: all round the eddies of Scamander .

sections.label: Section 1311
sections.original: σιγᾶτε σιγᾶτʼ · ᾐσθόμην κτύπου τινὸς
sections.translation: Hush ! hush ! I caught the sound of a foot-fall on the road near the house .

sections.label: Section 1313
sections.original: ὦ φίλταται γυναῖκες , ἐς μέσον φόνον
sections.translation: My dearest friends , it is Hermione advancing into the middle of the bloodshed ; let our clamor cease .

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: στείχει γὰρ ἐσπεσοῦσα δικτύων βρόχους .
sections.translation: For she comes headlong into the meshes of the net . The prey will be good , if it is caught . Take up your places again with looks composed and faces not betraying what has happened ; I too will have a gloomy look ,

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: ὡς δῆθεν οὐκ εἰδυῖα τἀξειργασμένα .
sections.translation: as if I knew nothing of what has been done . Addressing Hermione as she approaches . Ah ! maiden , have you come from wreathing Clytemnestra ’ s grave and pouring libations to the dead ?

sections.label: Section 1323
sections.original: ἥκω , λαβοῦσα πρευμένειαν . ἀλλά μοι
sections.translation: Yes , I have returned after securing her favor ; but I was filled with some alarm about a cry I heard from the palace

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: τηλουρὸς οὖσα δωμάτων κλύω βοήν .
sections.translation: as I was still at a distance .

sections.label: Section 1326
sections.original: τί δʼ ; ἄξιʼ ἡμῖν τυγχάνει στεναγμάτων .
sections.translation: But why ? Our present lot gives cause for groans .

sections.label: Section 1327
sections.original: εὔφημος ἴσθι · τί δὲ νεώτερον λέγεις ;
sections.translation: Oh , don ’ t say so ! What is your news ?

sections.label: Section 1328
sections.original: θανεῖν Ὀρέστην κἄμʼ ἔδοξε τῇδε γῇ .
sections.translation: Argos has sentenced Orestes and me to death .

sections.label: Section 1329
sections.original: μὴ δῆτʼ , ἐμοῦ γε συγγενεῖς πεφυκότας .
sections.translation: Oh no ! not my own relatives !

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: ἄραρʼ · ἀνάγκης δʼ ἐς ζυγὸν καθέσταμεν .
sections.translation: It is decreed ; we have put on the yoke of necessity .

sections.label: Section 1331
sections.original: ἦ τοῦδʼ ἕκατι καὶ βοὴ κατὰ στέγας ;
sections.translation: Was this the reason of the cry within ?

sections.label: Section 1332
sections.original: ἱκέτης γὰρ Ἑλένης γόνασι προσπεσὼν βοᾷ —
sections.translation: Yes , a suppliant cried out as he fell at Helen ’ s knees —

sections.label: Section 1333
sections.original: τίς ; οὐδὲν οἶδα μᾶλλον , ἢν σὺ μὴ λέγῃς .
sections.translation: Who is he ? I know nothing more , if you do not tell me .

sections.label: Section 1334
sections.original: τλήμων Ὀρέστης · μὴ θανεῖν , ἐμοῦ θʼ ὕπερ .
sections.translation: Unhappy Orestes , entreating mercy for himself and me .

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: ἐπʼ ἀξίοισί τἄρʼ ἀνευφημεῖ δόμος .
sections.translation: The house then has good reason to shout .

sections.label: Section 1336
sections.original: περὶ τοῦ γὰρ ἄλλου μᾶλλον ἂν φθέγξαιτό τις ;
sections.translation: What else would make someone entreat more earnestly ? But come and throw yourself before your mother in her prosperity , join your friends ’ supplication that Menelaus may not see us die .

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ τραφεῖσα μητρὸς ἐν χεροῖν ἐμῆς ,
sections.translation: O you that were nursed in my mother ’ s arms , have pity on us and relieve our pain . Come here to the struggle , and I myself will be your guide ; for you alone have power over our safety .

sections.label: Section 1344
sections.original: ἰδού , διώκω τὸν ἐμὸν ἐς δόμους πόδα .
sections.translation: See , I am hastening to the house ;

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: σώθηθʼ ὅσον γε τοὐπʼ ἐμέ .
sections.translation: as far it as rests with me , regard yourselves as safe . Exit Hermione .

sections.label: Section 1345b
sections.original: ὦ κατὰ στέγας
sections.translation: Now , friends in the house with swords , seize the prey !

sections.label: Section 1347
sections.original: οἲ ἐγώ · τίνας τούσδʼ εἰσορῶ ;
sections.translation: Oh no ! Who are these I see ?

sections.label: Section 1347b
sections.original: σιγᾶν χρεών ·
sections.translation: ( within .) Silence ! You are here for our safety , not yours .

sections.label: Section 1349
sections.original: ἔχεσθʼ ἔχεσθε · φάσγανον δὲ πρὸς δέρῃ
sections.translation: Hold her , hold her ! Point a sword at her throat ,

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: βάλλοντες ἡσυχάζεθʼ , ὡς εἰδῇ τόδε
sections.translation: then wait in silence , that Menelaus may learn that he has found men , not Phrygian cowards , and he has been treated as cowards deserve .

sections.label: Section 1.1353
sections.original: — ἰὼ ἰὼ φίλαι ,
sections.translation: Oh , oh , friends ! raise a din , a din and shouting before the house , that the murder when done

sections.label: Section 1.1355
sections.original: μὴ δεινὸν Ἀργείοισιν ἐμβάλῃ φόβον ,
sections.translation: may not inspire the Argives with wild alarm , to make them bring aid to the palace , before I see for certain that Helen ’ s corpse lies bloody in the house , or hear the news from one of her attendants ;

sections.label: Section 1.1360
sections.original: τὰ μὲν γὰρ οἶδα συμφορᾶς , τὰ δʼ οὐ σαφῶς .
sections.translation: for I know a part of the tragedy , of the rest I am not sure . In justice , retribution from the gods has come to Helen ; for she filled all Hellas with tears , through that accursed , accursed Paris of Ida ,

sections.label: Section 1.1365
sections.original: Πάριν , ὃς ἄγαγʼ Ἑλλάδʼ εἰς Ἴλιον .
sections.translation: who drew Hellas to Troy .

sections.label: Section 1366
sections.original: ἀλλὰ κτυπεῖ γὰρ κλῇθρα βασιλείων δόμων ,
sections.translation: But the bolts of the palace-doors rattle ; be silent ; for one of the Phrygians is coming out , from whom we will inquire of the state of matters within .

sections.label: Section 1369
sections.original: Ἀργέϊον ξίφος ἐκ θανάτου πέφευγα
sections.translation: ( expressing the most abject terror .) I have escaped from death by Argive sword ,

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: βαρβάροις ἐν εὐμάρι-
sections.translation: in my Asian slippers , by clambering over the cedar-beams that roof the porch and the Doric triglyphs , away , away ! O Earth , Earth ! in barbaric flight !
